title: Red — Karu (Ribbon)
lead: Lead your party with Crobat — and always bring a 6th 'sack' mon (≥ level 50) for the Legend Ribbon.

legend.meaning: Every one of Red's Pokémon uses a free move WHEN it enters (a buff, debuff, disruptor, or weather). Expect it.
Let Red knock out on purpose a mon that has done its job, to bring the next one in safely. The 6th 'sack' mon (≥ level 50) also earns the Legend Ribbon — even if it fainted.
Pivot moves: leave safely and bring the next mon in (also escape Block).
Move and Pokémon names stay in English to match the PokeMMO screen.

steps.text: You're here because Red opened with a Kanto mon (his lead is random). You lead with Crobat.
Sack rule: a mon that has done its job becomes a free-switch sacrifice to bring the next one in.
Venusaur: in Sun it's not a safe switch; otherwise it is.
Charizard: no safe switch — Glaceon becomes sackable after. If Charizard is Red's LEAD: sack mon 6, bring Glaceon, then immediately pivot to Conkeldurr (Red almost always switches to Snorlax).
Safety: Espeon, Charizard and Pikachu have no safe switch (the used mon becomes sackable). Snorlax is a safe switch. Blastoise: Ferrothorn's Power Whip only fails in crit range.
Red opened with Gliscor — Johto's fixed lead (Stealth Rock). Answer with Glaceon (Ice Beam). You still keep Crobat as your party lead.
Sack rule: a mon that has done its job becomes a free-switch sacrifice to bring the next one in.
Safety: Gliscor = safe switch. Typhlosion and Feraligatr = no safe switch (they become sacks). Tyranitar = safe switch, and DO switch (Conkeldurr stays on the field).

End of Johto: Pikachu will use Brick Break. Close it with priority — Conkeldurr Mach Punch + Glaceon Ice Shard; Ferrothorn's Iron Barbs covers low rolls. If Meganium lives, it uses Dragon Tail — react with priority/switch depending on who comes out.
Red opened with Swampert — Hoenn's fixed lead (Stealth Rock). You still keep Crobat as your party lead.
Hoenn has the most variance — Red may send his mons out of the expected order.
Sack rule: a mon that has done its job becomes a free-switch sacrifice to bring the next one in.
There are 2 versions: (1) Swampert immediately switches to Metagross, (2) Swampert stays. If it switches, U-Turn on Crobat to react.

rows.move: Ferrothorn — Spikes, depois Power Whip / Ferrothorn — Spikes, then Power Whip

steps.text: Swampert: use Ferrothorn to set Spikes. Do NOT swap Spikes for Stealth Rock — it changes what Red sends and jams the fight. Only this team's Pikachu survives Cross Poison; Spikes fix that.
Metagross: do NOT let Garchomp take damage from it before Blaziken is dead. Before Blaziken: Glaceon Shadow Ball → Garchomp EQ. After Blaziken: Garchomp EQ → Glaceon Shadow Ball.
Sceptile: it must NOT deal damage to Crobat. If Metagross hasn't come out yet, use U-Turn; if Metagross is dead, use Cross Poison.
Blaziken: the AI uses HP Ice on Garchomp; Assault Vest makes it switch to Close Combat. Keep Garchomp healthy if Blaziken shows up 5th.

Red opened with Staraptor — Sinnoh's fixed lead. It uses Whirlwind and forces a switch, but Red's own AI works around it, so this fight is one of the most consistent. You still keep Crobat as your party lead.
Sack rule: a mon that has done its job becomes a free-switch sacrifice to bring the next one in.
Safety: only Empoleon is a safe switch (Conkeldurr). Everything else becomes a sack — including sacking Ferrothorn on Infernape and mon 6 on Staraptor. Lots of sacks: that's expected.
Conkeldurr is built for Empoleon: 1 Speed point faster and enough bulk to escape crit range — that's why you can sack so much and still take the KO without taking one back.
You're here because Red opened with a Unova mon (random lead, like Kanto). You lead with Crobat.
Sack rule: a mon that has done its job becomes a free-switch sacrifice to bring the next one in.
Sack mon 6 on turn 1 if Red leads with Excadrill, Hydreigon or Emboar (and Garchomp hasn't been brought in).
Safety: Serperior and Samurott = safe switch (mind Samurott's Block). Everything else becomes a sack.
Excadrill: make sure it takes a sack-kill BEFORE Conkeldurr comes in — that way, after Mach Punch, it falls to Life Orb recoil.

Samurott: Block can trap Crobat before it gets Pikachu + Serperior. Samurott has Ice Beam and comes out early if Crobat uses Cross Poison on Serperior — use Brave Bird to avoid it. Keep mon 6 ≥ level 50 (otherwise the AI switches weirdly).
Answer Pikachu with Crobat (Cross Poison). Pikachu only leads in Kanto or Unova — the response is the same; identify the region by Red's next mons.

branch.prompt: Which region is it? (check Red's next mons)
